description_long: This sculpture depicts three standing human figures carved in high relief against a rectangular stone slab. The central figure is taller and wears a tall, conical headdress, flanked by two slightly shorter figures, each with distinct headdresses and attire. The figures are rendered with idealized, symmetrical proportions and stand close together, their arms at their sides, with the central figure's left foot slightly forward. The base of the sculpture features carved hieroglyphic inscriptions, suggesting an ancient Egyptian origin. The surface is smooth but shows subtle textural variations, likely from the carving process and age. The rear of the sculpture is a flat, unadorned stone slab, providing structural support.
description: A carved stone sculpture of three ancient Egyptian figures in high relief, with detailed headdresses and hieroglyphs on the base. The figures stand against a flat stone backing, with the central figure prominently featured.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing all three figures in detail, including their headdresses, facial features, and the hieroglyphic base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the relief and the thickness of the stone slab, with the figures' profiles visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, unadorned stone backing with no visible figures or inscriptions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profiles of the figures and the slab's thickness, mirroring view 1.
attribute_subject_matter: three ancient Egyptian human figures in high relief
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
